title: AST SpaceMobile Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Strategic Partnerships and Operations
text: AST SpaceMobile reported Q1 2026 results with revenue climbing to $14.7 million and management reaffirming full-year guidance of $150.0–$200.0 million, largely tied to mobile network partners and U.S. government work. Total operating expenses were $164.1 million, and net loss attributable to common stockholders was $191.0 million, or ($0.66) per share, representing a significant miss versus analyst expectations of -$0.21 per share. The company reaffirmed full-year 2026 revenue outlook supported by a commercial pipeline worth over $1.2 billion and recent government contract wins. AST SpaceMobile ended March 31, 2026 with approximately $3.5 billion in cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash, after raising over $1.0 billion in new debt and investing roughly $379.3 million in property, equipment and spectrum. Key operational updates include BlueBird 8, 9, and 10 scheduled for launch in mid-June, with 32 satellites in advanced stages of assembly.
